Flagging it rather than silently fixing it, since it's a separate concern outside this decision's scope.

## 0035 — tiered-agent test: full grid, no whole-arm early stop, shakeout pass first

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer (side A, full grid) + process-economist (side B, pilot+early-stop) opposing counsel; session-operator (unassigned, execution-realism lens) reframed both. Verifier reproduced every claim.
- Context: Issue #41 Comment 1/3 pre-registers a 4-arm (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus-solo, Tiered) x 8-task x 3-rep grid (~250-300 invocations, ISSUE-41.md:71), decision rule "adopt Tiered if delta vs Sonnet-solo >= -0.05 AND tokens <= 0.7x" (ISSUE-41.md:51), and an existing within-cell escalation ("add reps/tasks only where CI straddles the boundary; stop when unambiguous," ISSUE-41.md:49 = ADR 0019:17). Open question at execution time: run the full grid before analysis, or pilot the highest-signal arm and stop the WHOLE experiment early once the gate is decidable.

**Decision.** Execute the full pre-registered grid (4 arms x 8 tasks x 3-rep floor) before any headline analysis. Three provisions:
1. **No arm is skippable** — Haiku-solo/Opus-solo aren't gate inputs; they're the separately pre-registered frontier (ISSUE-41.md:48) and Opus-ceiling/"real prize" (ISSUE-41.md:51) deliverables. Inability to flip the Tiered-vs-Sonnet gate doesn't make them muda.
2. **Only early-stop retained: ADR 0019's within-cell escalation** — 3-rep floor per (task x arm) cell, add reps/tasks only where that cell's CI straddles the boundary. Caps additions above the floor; does NOT permit dropping an arm, a cell below 3 reps, or a whole-grid stop.
3. **Shakeout pass first (poka-yoke).** Tiered + Sonnet-solo on 1-2 pre-screen tasks, tagged `shakeout=true`, excluded from the verdict — gates only that each `agent()` call's `model:` matches its tier (#53) and the validator→worker loop terminates within the retry cap. Decision-record (`shakeout=false`) runs begin only after it passes.

**Justification.** Pilot-and-stop saves ~48 invocations only by reclassifying two pre-registered deliverables as muda post hoc — the "stopped once it looked good" survivorship pre-registration exists to prevent (ISSUE-41.md:27). `eval_verdict.py` has no sequential-stopping/alpha-spending controller (0 matches for alpha|sequential|stop|spend|interim across 318 lines; verified) — ADR 0019's own "no owned stopping controller" is accepted for bounded within-cell addition, not a grid-level halt with no correction for multiple interim looks. The cost lever pilot-and-stop wanted is already captured by provision 2.

**Assumptions.**
- [unverifiable] WEAKEST: the frontier + Opus-ceiling findings carry decision value even when the Tiered-vs-Sonnet headline is already clear. REOPEN-IF a completed run shows those outputs fed no adopt/config decision.
- [checkable] pending — gated on issue #41's experiment execution: final snapshot must hold ≥3 `shakeout=false` reps for all 4 arms on every pre-screen-surviving task.
- [verified] `eval_verdict.py` (318 lines) has zero matches for alpha|sequential|stop|spend|interim.

**Rejected alternatives.** Grid-level pilot-and-stop; full grid with no shakeout distinction; counting shakeout runs toward the record.

**Revisit triggers.** Frontier/ceiling outputs go unused → pre-register a headline-only fast path next time. A `shakeout=false` cell flips direction on added reps → reopen ADR 0019's escalation rule. `eval_verdict.py` gains a real stopping controller → revisit whether grid-level interim looks become defensible.

# 0035 — tiered-agent test: full grid, no whole-arm early stop, shakeout pass first

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer (side A, full grid) + process-economist (side B, pilot+early-stop) opposing counsel; session-operator (unassigned, execution-realism lens) reframed both. Verifier reproduced every claim.
- Context: Issue #41 Comment 1/3 pre-registers a 4-arm (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus-solo, Tiered) x 8-task x 3-rep grid (~250-300 invocations, ISSUE-41.md:71), decision rule "adopt Tiered if delta vs Sonnet-solo >= -0.05 AND tokens <= 0.7x" (ISSUE-41.md:51), and an existing within-cell escalation ("add reps/tasks only where CI straddles the boundary; stop when unambiguous," ISSUE-41.md:49 = ADR 0019:17). Open question at execution time: run the full grid before analysis, or pilot the highest-signal arm and stop the WHOLE experiment early once the gate is decidable.

## Decision
Execute the full pre-registered grid (4 arms x 8 tasks x 3-rep floor) before any headline analysis. Three provisions:
1. **No arm is skippable** — Haiku-solo/Opus-solo aren't gate inputs; they're the separately pre-registered frontier (ISSUE-41.md:48) and Opus-ceiling/"real prize" (ISSUE-41.md:51) deliverables. Inability to flip the Tiered-vs-Sonnet gate doesn't make them muda.
2. **Only early-stop retained: ADR 0019's within-cell escalation** — 3-rep floor per (task x arm) cell, add reps/tasks only where that cell's CI straddles the boundary. Caps additions above the floor; does NOT permit dropping an arm, a cell below 3 reps, or a whole-grid stop.
3. **Shakeout pass first (poka-yoke).** Tiered + Sonnet-solo on 1-2 pre-screen tasks, tagged `shakeout=true`, excluded from the verdict — gates only that each `agent()` call's `model:` matches its tier (#53) and the validator->worker loop terminates within the retry cap. Decision-record (`shakeout=false`) runs begin only after it passes.

## Justification
Pilot-and-stop (rejected) saves ~48 invocations only by reclassifying two pre-registered deliverables as muda post hoc — the "stopped once it looked good" survivorship pre-registration exists to prevent (ISSUE-41.md:27). `eval_verdict.py` has no sequential-stopping/alpha-spending controller (0 matches for alpha|sequential|stop|spend|interim across 318 lines; verified) — ADR 0019's own "no owned stopping controller" (0019:17) is accepted for bounded within-cell addition, not a grid-level halt with no correction for multiple interim looks. The cost lever pilot-and-stop wanted is already captured by provision 2. Prefer poka-yoke over review: the `shakeout` flag + all-four-arms floor make "buggy orchestration contaminated the record" and "wrong model ran silently" (#53) impossible to commit, not merely reviewable.

## Assumptions
- [unverifiable] WEAKEST: the frontier + Opus-ceiling findings carry decision value even in worlds where the Tiered-vs-Sonnet headline is already clear — i.e. "adopt tiering?" isn't the only question this grid answers. REOPEN-IF a completed run shows those outputs fed no adopt/config decision — pre-register a headline-only fast path next time.
- [checkable] pending — gated on issue #41's experiment execution: the final append-only snapshot must hold >=3 `shakeout=false` reps for all 4 arms on every pre-screen-surviving task (JSONL count, no arm at zero). Owner: analysis-time gate.
- [verified] `eval_verdict.py` (318 lines) has zero matches for alpha|sequential|stop|spend|interim — grep, this session.

## Rejected alternatives
- Grid-level pilot-and-stop — generalizes cell escalation to whole-arm skipping with no pre-registered boundary or alpha-spending correction; forfeits frontier/ceiling deliverables.
- Full grid with no shakeout distinction — lets the first run of unexercised orchestration code enter the verdict; a `model:` omission (#53) would silently spend ~250-300 invocations on the wrong tier.
- Shakeout runs counted toward the record — teaches to a buggy harness.

## Revisit triggers
- Frontier/ceiling outputs go unused in the adopt decision -> pre-register a headline-only fast path.
- A `shakeout=false` cell flips direction on added within-cell reps -> reopen ADR 0019's escalation rule (0019:43).
- `eval_verdict.py` gains a real stopping controller -> revisit whether grid-level interim looks become defensible.
